mechanism_narrative: When the effective federal funds rate exceeds a restrictive threshold of 5%, higher borrowing costs compress corporate margins and increase the discount rate for future earnings, leading to negative equity returns over the medium term as the policy's effects transmit through the economy.
evidence_refs: fact:Current FEDFUNDS is 5.33, above the trigger threshold.
failure_condition: The S&P 500 exhibits a statistically significant positive return drift in the 60 trading days following instances where FEDFUNDS crosses above 5%.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) reflects a robust and expanding economy. This strength translates directly into higher aggregate demand and corporate revenue expectations, driving positive equity returns.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS is a widely followed coincident indicator of economic health.
failure_condition: A rolling 36-month correlation between the 3-month change in PAYEMS and 20-day forward S&P 500 returns is consistently negative or statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: The simultaneous occurrence of slowing real retail sales growth and rising unemployment signals a sharp contraction in consumer health. This joint signal indicates a high probability of a near-term recession, leading to a broad-based sell-off in equities.
candidate_trigger: Year-over-year % change in RSAFS < 2% AND 3-month change in UNRATE > 0.3pp
failure_condition: In periods where the trigger condition is met, the S&P 500 fails to produce negative returns on average over the subsequent 60 trading days.

mechanism_narrative: A downside surprise in the monthly CPI reading during a period of high interest rates (FEDFUNDS > 4%) increases the market-perceived probability of an earlier-than-expected monetary policy pivot towards easing. This repricing of the discount rate path drives positive equity returns.
candidate_trigger: Month-over-month change in CPIAUCSL is below consensus forecast
evidence_refs: fact:Recent CPI prints have shown signs of disinflation.
failure_condition: The average 20-day S&P 500 return following downside CPI surprises (when FEDFUNDS > 4%) is not statistically different from the average return following upside surprises.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production growth indicates a healthy manufacturing and supply side of the economy. This strength in output is a leading indicator of corporate earnings power, driving positive short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: The rank information coefficient between the 6-month change in INDPRO and 20-day forward S&P 500 returns is less than 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate, as defined by the Sahm Rule trigger (3-month average of UNRATE rising 0.5pp above its 12-month low), is a highly reliable real-time indicator of recession. This signal precedes sharp declines in corporate earnings and equity valuations.
candidate_trigger: 3-month avg of UNRATE rises > 0.5pp from its 12-month low
failure_condition: The S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of instances following the activation of the Sahm Rule trigger.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in housing starts (HOUST) signals falling consumer confidence and investment, acting as a leading indicator for a broader economic slowdown. This negative signal for future growth prospects weighs on equity market returns.
evidence_refs: fact:Housing is a highly interest-rate sensitive sector.
failure_condition: The average 60-day S&P 500 return following a trigger event is not statistically significantly negative compared to the unconditional average return.

mechanism_narrative: An upside surprise in the monthly CPI inflation report forces an immediate repricing of the expected path of monetary policy toward a more hawkish stance. This sudden increase in expected future interest rates causes a negative shock to equity valuations over a very short horizon.
candidate_trigger: Released CPIAUCSL is >0.2% above median economist forecast
evidence_refs: fact:Equity markets have shown high sensitivity to inflation data releases in 2022-2023.
failure_condition: The average 5-day S&P 500 return following significant upside CPI surprises is non-negative over a multi-year sample.
